name: Radio
summary: A single radio option rendered inside a `RadioGroup`; author it as a `RadioGroup` child rather than standalone — the group owns selection, keyboard roving focus, and form binding.

props.name: preset
props.description: One or many style-preset names registered via `definePreset()`

bestPractices: Label the group clearly. Provide a visible prompt (e.g., a preceding `Typography` ) and ensure `name` is set so the group behaves as a single form field.
Choose controlled vs uncontrolled deliberately. Use `value` / `onValueChange` when the selection drives app state; otherwise, `defaultValue` is sufficient.

Binding precedence (prop > form > internal). An explicit `value` prop always wins; otherwise a `name` inside a `FormControl` binds to the store; otherwise the group is uncontrolled. The binding is latched at mount.

Form binding reads and writes. When bound by `name`, the group both reads the store (a seeded value drives the initial selection and `form.reset()` is reflected) and writes selections back. Seed the key in `createFormStore(initial)` so the initial selection and reset behave as expected.

Change source. `onValueChange`/`onValueCommit` report a truthful `info.source`: `'pointer'` for a mouse selection and `'keyboard'` for keyboard activation (arrow roving, Home/End, Space/Enter), not a blanket `'programmatic'`.

Set option spacing with `gap`. RadioGroup's `gap` controls the spacing between options (theme spacing units or a CSS length). The old `spacing` prop is a deprecated alias (Q12): it keeps working through 0.x with a one-time dev warning and is removed at 1.0 — migrate to `gap`, which wins when both are supplied.
Align to available space. Use `row` for short option lists in wide layouts; prefer a column for long labels or narrow screens.
Keep targets comfortable. Use token sizes ( `'sm'` .. `'xl'` ) or numeric values to maintain touch targets ≥ 40–48px depending on density.
Avoid nesting interactive content inside labels. Keep each option’s label succinct and non-interactive so keyboard and screen reader navigation remain predictable.
Use presets for visual variants. Capture chip/pill styles with `definePreset` and apply via `preset` so branding and states stay consistent.

examples.title: Radio options in a RadioGroup
